title: Public-submission rank remap and gated micro-intervention blender
summary: When only public submission files are available, treat high-scoring predictions as rank sources, remap unusual distributions onto a trusted anchor, and apply tightly gated corrections instead of broad averaging.
rationale: Multiple high-vote blender notebooks converge on the same pattern: direct probability averaging works only for similar distributions, while irregular high-scoring sources such as the 0.95446 file should be used as ordering signals. The richer notebooks add logit-rank remaps, confidence gates, micro-band rescaling, and tail-only max/min interventions for ROC-AUC.
implementation_hint: Build a submission-ingestion script that validates row IDs, clips probabilities, computes pairwise correlations and rank correlations, identifies an anchor, and tests: small direct blends, rank remap, logit-rank remap, middle-only gates, tail-only OR/AND rank interventions, and percentile micro-band rescaling. Keep a report of changed-row counts because no OOF target exists for pure public submissions.
expected_effect: Can extract leaderboard-only micro gains from diverse public submissions without destroying the anchor distribution.
risk: No honest local validation when only submissions are available; this should be tracked separately from model hypotheses and can overfit public LB noise.
